romania.title: SUA acuză DeepSeek și alte companii chineze de AI că ar fura tehnologie americană. Avertisment global al Departamentului de Stat
romania.summary: Departamentul de Stat al SUA a lansat o acțiune globală prin care cere diplomaților americani să atragă atenția asupra presupuselor practici ale unor companii chineze de inteligență artificială, inclusiv DeepSeek, acuzate că ar încerca să obțină tehnologie și proprietate intelectuală din Statele Unite, potrivit unei telegrame diplomatice citate de Reuters.

romania.title: 😂 Dilemă pentru un drojdier: din ajutorul social să-și ia băutură sau acțiuni la Hidroelectrica?
romania.summary: Stres mare pentru un drojdier român generic, care a observat că planul Guvernului de a lista la Bursă companii mari de stat, precum Hidroelectrica, este o oportunitate majoră de investiții. Drojdierul deja analizează dacă luna viitoare să-și ia băutură din ajutorul social sau să-și sune brokerul și să-i solicite un ”buy” ferm, mai ales că […] The post Dilemă pentru un drojdier: din ajutorul social să-și ia băutură sau acțiuni la Hidroelectrica? appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: Șofer înjunghiat de un taximetrist într-o staţie GPL din București. Agresorul s-ar fi enervat că bărbatul a întârziat când a plătit
romania.summary: Un bărbat de 36 de ani a fost transportat la spital, vineri, după ce a fost înjunghiat în picior de un taximetrist, într-o staţie GPL din Sectorul 6 al Capitalei. Între cei doi a izbucnit o ceartă, taximetristul fiind nemulţumit că bărbatul a întârziat când a plătit, întrucât a cumpărat şi alte produse. Taximetristul este audiat de poliţişti.

romania.summary: Programul național Telefonul Vârstnicului – 0800 460 001 Sprijinul media a venit de la Spot TV – Pro TV, TVR, Antena 1, Antena 3 CNN, Antena Stars, Prima TV, Prima News, Pro Cinema, Pro Arena, Acasă, Acasă Gold, Trinitas TV, Speranța TV, Antena 3 Deva,... Inițiativa a vizat îmbunătățirea sănătății… Articolul Prima ediție a Caravanei Pașilor Mici ajunge la final: peste 200 de beneficiari deserviți și alte câteva mii care așteaptă sprijin în comunitățile rurale din Iași apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.summary: Cu câteva luni înainte de ediția cu numărul 14, Maratonul Olteniei, un eveniment Distribuție Oltenia, primește o nouă recunoaștere internațională: campania „Maratonul Optimismului”, derulată în 2025, este câștigătoare la categoria… Articolul Maratonul Olteniei: un eveniment născut la Râmnicu Vâlcea, premiat internațional apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.summary: Publicul din București, Constanța, Alba Iulia, Timișoara și Câmpina este invitat, în perioada 11–13 mai, la o serie de spectacole de teatru care reunesc nume importante ale scenei românești. De… Articolul „Fierarii”, „Nelson” și „Fă-mi loc”, spectacolele săptămânii în București și în mai multe orașe din țară apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: 😂 Lovitură pentru Grindeanu! CCR a anulat referendumul intern PSD
romania.summary: Înfrângere totală pentru liderul PSD Sorin Grindeanu! Curtea Constituțională a anulat referendumul intern organizat lunea trecută de PSD, și care a declanșat această criză politică și guvernamentală. CCR susține că referendumul nu poate fi validat pentru că nu au fost observatori în niciuna dintre secțiile de vot din sediile PSD, iar cei care au votat […] The post Lovitură pentru Grindeanu! CCR a anulat referendumul intern PSD appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: 😂 Wizz Air anunţă că nu scumpeşte biletele, dar le va cere pasagerilor câte 10 litri de kerosen la check-in
romania.summary: Veşti bune pentru pasagerii companiilor aeriene low-cost! Wizz Air a anunţat că va păstra neschimbate preţurile biletelor pentru majoritatea destinaţiilor, impunându-le călătorilor o singură condiţie: să aducă la check-in un bidon, butoiaş, găleată sau damigeană cu 10 litri de kerosen. „La întoarcere, pasagerii îşi vor putea recupera bidonul de la acelaşi birou de check-in, iar […] The post Wizz Air anunţă că nu scumpeşte biletele, dar le va cere pasagerilor câte 10 litri de kerosen la check-in appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
romania.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
romania.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

romania.summary: De ce vorbim despre bullying Bullyingul nu mai este de mult o problemă „minoră” sau o etapă inevitabilă a copilăriei.... Articolul Bullyingul în adolescență: răni invizibile și cum pot fi vindecate apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: Descrierea postului: Părintele social crează un mediu de viaţă, îngrijire şi educaţie, caracterizat prin afecţiune şi stabilitate şi adecvat dezvoltării personalităţii fiecărui copil (în plan fizic, psihic, emoţional, social şi... romania.source: Hope and Homes for Children Romania
romania.title: „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj
romania.summary: Hope and Homes for Children, alături de Distribuție Oltenia, parte din Evryo, a derulat campania „Grijă pentru mine”, un proiect dedicat sănătății emoționale aadolescentelor din medii vulnerabile. Inițiativa a ajuns la peste 250 de fete din județul Gorj, inclusiv tinere din sistemul de protecție și din comunități rurale, oferindu-le sprijin pentru a-și înțelege emoțiile, a-șicrește... Articolul „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj apare prima dată în Hope and Homes for Children .

romania.title: De la un proiect Teach for Romania la o inițiativă europeană: cum elevii din Reghin construiesc „Leadership HUB”
romania.summary: O idee pornită din dorința de a dezvolta competențele de leadership ale elevilor s-a transformat, în doar câțiva ani, într-o inițiativă educațională care conectează școli din mai multe țări europene. Povestea proiectului „Leadership HUB” începe cu un proiect finanțat de Teach for Romania și continuă astăzi prin colaborări internaționale în cadrul platformei eTwinning și al […]

medical.title: Growing support for AI models in heart disease care and prevention
medical.summary: Digital transformation and artificial intelligence (AI) in health care requires a range of safeguards and standards to work well, but new research from Flinders University provides support for effective AI systems to improve cardiovascular care. The study examines how Clinical Decision Support Systems (CDSS) can transform cardiovascular disease management—a leading cause of death in Australia—by providing more accurate, timely decisions while addressing real-world barriers like workflow integration, usability and clinician adoption.

medical.title: Evaluating effectiveness of maternal RSV vaccination and nirsevimab for severe RSV-related illness in infants
medical.summary: A new study found that infants receiving maternal Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) vaccination or Nirsevimab after birth had significantly lower RSV infection and health care use compared to unprotected infants. It also showed fewer severe complications in RSV-positive infants with prevention. These findings highlight the positive impact of RSV preventive measures in high-risk populations. Findings from the study will be presented at the Pediatric Academic Societies (PAS) 2026 Meeting, taking place April 24–27 in Boston.

medical.title: Results from COSMOS trial reveals more than 75% of older adults have used complementary therapies
medical.summary: A new study by investigators from Mass General Brigham suggests that complementary health approaches (CHA)—including massage, yoga, tai chi, herbal products, acupuncture, spiritual practices, cannabis and psychedelics—are now a routine part of health and wellness for many older adults, even though they are not always discussed with health care providers

medical.title: Novel blood return system cuts blood loss by 97% in pulmonary embolism procedure
medical.summary: The first comparative analysis from the ENGULF (A Safety and Feasibility Single-Arm Study of a Novel Catheter Thrombectomy Device For the Treatment of Pulmonary Embolism) trial shows that using blood return during continuous aspiration thrombectomy dramatically reduces blood loss by 97%, while maintaining hemodynamic efficacy and improving procedural efficiency.

medical.title: US dentists still prescribe far more opioids for pain than peer nations
medical.summary: People getting their teeth pulled or drilled by dentists in the United States are still much more likely to get powerful opioid medications than dental patients in other developed countries or even the U.S. territory of Puerto Rico, a new study finds.

medical.title: Maternal dairy intake consistent with recommendations linked to lower levels of specific human milk lipids
medical.summary: Results from a pilot study of 100 exclusively breastfeeding women suggest that maternal dairy intake is associated with decreased concentrations of numerous human milk lipids. Findings from the study will be presented at the Pediatric Academic Societies (PAS) 2026 Meeting, taking place April 24–27 in Boston.

medical.title: Scientists discover why bread can cause weight gain without extra calories
medical.summary: Bread and other carbohydrate staples may be doing more than just filling plates—they could be quietly reshaping metabolism. In a surprising twist, researchers found that mice strongly preferred carbs like bread, rice, and wheat, abandoning their regular diet entirely. Even without eating more calories, they gained weight and body fat, not because they overate, but because their bodies burned less energy.

medical.title: What does the new £1bn investment in community energy really mean?
medical.summary: It’s the largest public investment in community-owned green power in the UK to date. But how can communities get involved? The post What does the new £1bn investment in community energy really mean? appeared first on Positive News .

medical.title: Blocking a single protein supercharges the immune system against cancer
medical.summary: Scientists have discovered a way to supercharge the immune system’s T cells by blocking a protein called Ant2, forcing the cells to rewire how they generate energy. This shift makes them more powerful, resilient, and effective at finding and destroying cancer cells.

medical.title: Lonely people have worse memory but don’t decline faster, study finds
medical.summary: Loneliness may quietly affect how well older adults remember things—but it might not be speeding up mental decline after all. A large European study tracking over 10,000 people for seven years found that those who felt lonelier started off with weaker memory, yet their memory didn’t deteriorate any faster than those who felt more socially connected. The findings challenge the idea that loneliness directly accelerates cognitive decline or dementia, suggesting instead that it impacts baseline brain performance.

medical.title: This strange “pearling” motion inside cells could change how we understand disease
medical.summary: Mitochondria don’t just generate energy—they also carefully organize their own DNA in a surprisingly elegant way. Scientists have discovered that a long-overlooked phenomenon called “mitochondrial pearling,” where mitochondria briefly form bead-like shapes, helps evenly space clusters of mitochondrial DNA.

medical.title: The people you live with could be changing your gut bacteria
medical.summary: Spending time with close companions might do more than strengthen bonds—it could also reshape your gut bacteria. In a study of island birds, those with stronger social ties shared more gut microbes, especially types that require direct contact to spread. This suggests that social interaction itself—not just shared space—drives microbial exchange. The same process may be happening in human households through everyday closeness.

science.title: 42 lost pages of the new testament manuscript discovered
science.summary: An international team of academics led by Professor Garrick Allen at the University of Glasgow has successfully recovered 42 lost pages from one of the world's most important early New Testament manuscripts: Codex H.

science.title: Amazon recovery masks diversity loss as fires, droughts and windstorms reshape forest edges
science.summary: Even after fires, severe droughts, and windstorms, the vegetation in degraded Amazonian forests demonstrates a high capacity for regeneration, including tree species. However, recovery occurs under new ecological conditions, resulting in a loss of diversity and increased vulnerability to new disturbances.

science.title: First gap-free peanut genomes reveal genes behind bigger seeds and better oils
science.summary: An international team of researchers has produced complete, gap-free genome sequences for six peanut varieties, providing a comprehensive blueprint for future peanut breeding and improvement strategies.

science.title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
science.summary: This shimmering region of star-formation, a close-up of the Trifid Nebula about 5,000 light-years from Earth, was captured in intricate detail by NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope in an image released on April 20, 2026. The colors in Hubble’s visible light image, which marks the 36th anniversary of the mission’s launch on April 24, are reminiscent of an underwater […]

science.title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
science.summary: Students in Missouri will hear from NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway as they answer prerecorded science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) questions while aboard the International Space Station. The Earth-to-space call will begin at 10:50 a.m. EDT Thursday, April 30, and will stream live on the agency’s Learn With NASA YouTube channel. This […]

science.title: New approach to detect ultra-rare part-per-sextillion isotopes could also sharpen dark matter searches
science.summary: The detection and study of isotopes, atoms of the same element that have different numbers of neutrons, could expand the scope of physics research and enable new scientific discoveries. So far, rare isotopes have been primarily detected using a technique known as accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS), which accelerates atoms, to then measure their mass and charge.

science.title: Mammal ancestors laid eggs, and this 250-million-year-old fossil finally proves it
science.summary: In the aftermath of Earth’s most catastrophic extinction event, one unlikely survivor rose to dominate a shattered world: Lystrosaurus. Now, a stunning fossil discovery—an ancient egg containing a curled-up embryo—has finally answered a decades-old mystery about whether mammal ancestors laid eggs. Using advanced imaging technology, scientists confirmed that these resilient creatures did reproduce this way, likely producing large, soft-shelled eggs packed with nutrients.

science.title: Quantum systems can remember and forget at the same time, scientists discover
science.summary: Quantum systems can secretly “remember” their past—even when they appear not to. Scientists found that whether a system shows memory depends on how you look at it: through its evolving state or its measurable properties. Each perspective uncovers different kinds of memory, meaning a system can seem memoryless and memory-filled at the same time. This discovery could change how researchers design and control quantum technologies.

environment.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
environment.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

environment.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
environment.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

environment.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
environment.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

environment.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
environment.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

environment.title: Lake Coatepeque
environment.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

environment.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
environment.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

environment.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
environment.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

environment.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
environment.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

mix_items.title: „MARILE TRECERI”: spectacol multimedia prezentat de Corul de Copii Radio
mix_items.summary: Corul de Copii Radio, o pepinieră de talente și un adevărat ambasador cultural al României, ansamblu ce a împlinit frumoasa vârstă de 80 de ani în 2025, ne invită la… Articolul „MARILE TRECERI”: spectacol multimedia prezentat de Corul de Copii Radio apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

mix_items.title: „Fierarii”, „Nelson” și „Fă-mi loc”, spectacolele săptămânii în București și în mai multe orașe din țară
mix_items.summary: Publicul din București, Constanța, Alba Iulia, Timișoara și Câmpina este invitat, în perioada 11–13 mai, la o serie de spectacole de teatru care reunesc nume importante ale scenei românești. De… Articolul „Fierarii”, „Nelson” și „Fă-mi loc”, spectacolele săptămânii în București și în mai multe orașe din țară apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

mix_items.title: 😂 Wizz Air anunţă că nu scumpeşte biletele, dar le va cere pasagerilor câte 10 litri de kerosen la check-in
mix_items.summary: Veşti bune pentru pasagerii companiilor aeriene low-cost! Wizz Air a anunţat că va păstra neschimbate preţurile biletelor pentru majoritatea destinaţiilor, impunându-le călătorilor o singură condiţie: să aducă la check-in un bidon, butoiaş, găleată sau damigeană cu 10 litri de kerosen. „La întoarcere, pasagerii îşi vor putea recupera bidonul de la acelaşi birou de check-in, iar […] The post Wizz Air anunţă că nu scumpeşte biletele, dar le va cere pasagerilor câte 10 litri de kerosen la check-in appeared first on Times New Roman .

mix_items.title: Șofer înjunghiat de un taximetrist într-o staţie GPL din București. Agresorul s-ar fi enervat că bărbatul a întârziat când a plătit
mix_items.summary: Un bărbat de 36 de ani a fost transportat la spital, vineri, după ce a fost înjunghiat în picior de un taximetrist, într-o staţie GPL din Sectorul 6 al Capitalei. Între cei doi a izbucnit o ceartă, taximetristul fiind nemulţumit că bărbatul a întârziat când a plătit, întrucât a cumpărat şi alte produse. Taximetristul este audiat de poliţişti.

mix_items.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
mix_items.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas
mix_items.summary: Un drum construit prin educație Simona este elevă în clasa a XI-a, la specializarea Tehnician în industria alimentară, la un... Articolul Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale
mix_items.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Psiholog
mix_items.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: 42 lost pages of the new testament manuscript discovered
mix_items.summary: An international team of academics led by Professor Garrick Allen at the University of Glasgow has successfully recovered 42 lost pages from one of the world's most important early New Testament manuscripts: Codex H.

mix_items.title: „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase.
mix_items.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
mix_items.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Growing support for AI models in heart disease care and prevention
mix_items.summary: Digital transformation and artificial intelligence (AI) in health care requires a range of safeguards and standards to work well, but new research from Flinders University provides support for effective AI systems to improve cardiovascular care. The study examines how Clinical Decision Support Systems (CDSS) can transform cardiovascular disease management—a leading cause of death in Australia—by providing more accurate, timely decisions while addressing real-world barriers like workflow integration, usability and clinician adoption.

mix_items.title: Bullyingul în adolescență: răni invizibile și cum pot fi vindecate
mix_items.summary: De ce vorbim despre bullying Bullyingul nu mai este de mult o problemă „minoră” sau o etapă inevitabilă a copilăriei.... Articolul Bullyingul în adolescență: răni invizibile și cum pot fi vindecate apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Părinte Social
mix_items.summary: Descrierea postului: Părintele social crează un mediu de viaţă, îngrijire şi educaţie, caracterizat prin afecţiune şi stabilitate şi adecvat dezvoltării personalităţii fiecărui copil (în plan fizic, psihic, emoţional, social şi... The post Părinte Social appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: Evaluating effectiveness of maternal RSV vaccination and nirsevimab for severe RSV-related illness in infants
mix_items.summary: A new study found that infants receiving maternal Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) vaccination or Nirsevimab after birth had significantly lower RSV infection and health care use compared to unprotected infants. It also showed fewer severe complications in RSV-positive infants with prevention. These findings highlight the positive impact of RSV preventive measures in high-risk populations. Findings from the study will be presented at the Pediatric Academic Societies (PAS) 2026 Meeting, taking place April 24–27 in Boston.

mix_items.source: Hope and Homes for Children Romania
mix_items.title: „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj
mix_items.summary: Hope and Homes for Children, alături de Distribuție Oltenia, parte din Evryo, a derulat campania „Grijă pentru mine”, un proiect dedicat sănătății emoționale aadolescentelor din medii vulnerabile. Inițiativa a ajuns la peste 250 de fete din județul Gorj, inclusiv tinere din sistemul de protecție și din comunități rurale, oferindu-le sprijin pentru a-și înțelege emoțiile, a-șicrește... Articolul „Grijă pentru mine” – campanie dedicată adolescentelor din Gorj apare prima dată în Hope and Homes for Children .

mix_items.title: De la un proiect Teach for Romania la o inițiativă europeană: cum elevii din Reghin construiesc „Leadership HUB”
mix_items.summary: O idee pornită din dorința de a dezvolta competențele de leadership ale elevilor s-a transformat, în doar câțiva ani, într-o inițiativă educațională care conectează școli din mai multe țări europene. Povestea proiectului „Leadership HUB” începe cu un proiect finanțat de Teach for Romania și continuă astăzi prin colaborări internaționale în cadrul platformei eTwinning și al […]

mix_items.title: Amazon recovery masks diversity loss as fires, droughts and windstorms reshape forest edges
mix_items.summary: Even after fires, severe droughts, and windstorms, the vegetation in degraded Amazonian forests demonstrates a high capacity for regeneration, including tree species. However, recovery occurs under new ecological conditions, resulting in a loss of diversity and increased vulnerability to new disturbances.

mix_items.title: Results from COSMOS trial reveals more than 75% of older adults have used complementary therapies
mix_items.summary: A new study by investigators from Mass General Brigham suggests that complementary health approaches (CHA)—including massage, yoga, tai chi, herbal products, acupuncture, spiritual practices, cannabis and psychedelics—are now a routine part of health and wellness for many older adults, even though they are not always discussed with health care providers

mix_items.title: Novel blood return system cuts blood loss by 97% in pulmonary embolism procedure
mix_items.summary: The first comparative analysis from the ENGULF (A Safety and Feasibility Single-Arm Study of a Novel Catheter Thrombectomy Device For the Treatment of Pulmonary Embolism) trial shows that using blood return during continuous aspiration thrombectomy dramatically reduces blood loss by 97%, while maintaining hemodynamic efficacy and improving procedural efficiency.

mix_items.title: First gap-free peanut genomes reveal genes behind bigger seeds and better oils
mix_items.summary: An international team of researchers has produced complete, gap-free genome sequences for six peanut varieties, providing a comprehensive blueprint for future peanut breeding and improvement strategies.

mix_items.title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
mix_items.summary: This shimmering region of star-formation, a close-up of the Trifid Nebula about 5,000 light-years from Earth, was captured in intricate detail by NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope in an image released on April 20, 2026. The colors in Hubble’s visible light image, which marks the 36th anniversary of the mission’s launch on April 24, are reminiscent of an underwater […]

mix_items.title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
mix_items.summary: Students in Missouri will hear from NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway as they answer prerecorded science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) questions while aboard the International Space Station. The Earth-to-space call will begin at 10:50 a.m. EDT Thursday, April 30, and will stream live on the agency’s Learn With NASA YouTube channel. This […]

mix_items.title: New approach to detect ultra-rare part-per-sextillion isotopes could also sharpen dark matter searches
mix_items.summary: The detection and study of isotopes, atoms of the same element that have different numbers of neutrons, could expand the scope of physics research and enable new scientific discoveries. So far, rare isotopes have been primarily detected using a technique known as accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS), which accelerates atoms, to then measure their mass and charge.

mix_items.title: US dentists still prescribe far more opioids for pain than peer nations
mix_items.summary: People getting their teeth pulled or drilled by dentists in the United States are still much more likely to get powerful opioid medications than dental patients in other developed countries or even the U.S. territory of Puerto Rico, a new study finds.

mix_items.title: Maternal dairy intake consistent with recommendations linked to lower levels of specific human milk lipids
mix_items.summary: Results from a pilot study of 100 exclusively breastfeeding women suggest that maternal dairy intake is associated with decreased concentrations of numerous human milk lipids. Findings from the study will be presented at the Pediatric Academic Societies (PAS) 2026 Meeting, taking place April 24–27 in Boston.

mix_items.title: Mammal ancestors laid eggs, and this 250-million-year-old fossil finally proves it
mix_items.summary: In the aftermath of Earth’s most catastrophic extinction event, one unlikely survivor rose to dominate a shattered world: Lystrosaurus. Now, a stunning fossil discovery—an ancient egg containing a curled-up embryo—has finally answered a decades-old mystery about whether mammal ancestors laid eggs. Using advanced imaging technology, scientists confirmed that these resilient creatures did reproduce this way, likely producing large, soft-shelled eggs packed with nutrients.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover why bread can cause weight gain without extra calories
mix_items.summary: Bread and other carbohydrate staples may be doing more than just filling plates—they could be quietly reshaping metabolism. In a surprising twist, researchers found that mice strongly preferred carbs like bread, rice, and wheat, abandoning their regular diet entirely. Even without eating more calories, they gained weight and body fat, not because they overate, but because their bodies burned less energy.

mix_items.title: What does the new £1bn investment in community energy really mean?
mix_items.summary: It’s the largest public investment in community-owned green power in the UK to date. But how can communities get involved? The post What does the new £1bn investment in community energy really mean? appeared first on Positive News .

mix_items.title: Blocking a single protein supercharges the immune system against cancer
mix_items.summary: Scientists have discovered a way to supercharge the immune system’s T cells by blocking a protein called Ant2, forcing the cells to rewire how they generate energy. This shift makes them more powerful, resilient, and effective at finding and destroying cancer cells.

mix_items.title: Lonely people have worse memory but don’t decline faster, study finds
mix_items.summary: Loneliness may quietly affect how well older adults remember things—but it might not be speeding up mental decline after all. A large European study tracking over 10,000 people for seven years found that those who felt lonelier started off with weaker memory, yet their memory didn’t deteriorate any faster than those who felt more socially connected. The findings challenge the idea that loneliness directly accelerates cognitive decline or dementia, suggesting instead that it impacts baseline brain performance.

mix_items.title: Quantum systems can remember and forget at the same time, scientists discover
mix_items.summary: Quantum systems can secretly “remember” their past—even when they appear not to. Scientists found that whether a system shows memory depends on how you look at it: through its evolving state or its measurable properties. Each perspective uncovers different kinds of memory, meaning a system can seem memoryless and memory-filled at the same time. This discovery could change how researchers design and control quantum technologies.

mix_items.title: This strange “pearling” motion inside cells could change how we understand disease
mix_items.summary: Mitochondria don’t just generate energy—they also carefully organize their own DNA in a surprisingly elegant way. Scientists have discovered that a long-overlooked phenomenon called “mitochondrial pearling,” where mitochondria briefly form bead-like shapes, helps evenly space clusters of mitochondrial DNA.

mix_items.title: The people you live with could be changing your gut bacteria
mix_items.summary: Spending time with close companions might do more than strengthen bonds—it could also reshape your gut bacteria. In a study of island birds, those with stronger social ties shared more gut microbes, especially types that require direct contact to spread. This suggests that social interaction itself—not just shared space—drives microbial exchange. The same process may be happening in human households through everyday closeness.

mix_items.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
mix_items.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
mix_items.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

mix_items.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
mix_items.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
mix_items.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

mix_items.title: Lake Coatepeque
mix_items.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

mix_items.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
mix_items.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
mix_items.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

mix_items.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
mix_items.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

joke_ro.text: Mi-am facut meal prep. Am pregatit mental ideea.

satire_ro.title: Dilemă pentru un drojdier: din ajutorul social să-și ia băutură sau acțiuni la Hidroelectrica?
